It does not involve task-based activation or problem-solving in healthy adults and appears to be a non-fMRI perfusion methodology paper.

The study is about PET image reconstruction/scatter correction and evaluates dynamic PET in non-human primates and phantoms. It is not an fMRI study and does not include healthy adult human participants performing problem-solving tasks.

This is an engineering/physics study about a PET detector and GPU-based optical simulation; it is not an fMRI study and involves no human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Does not meet the review objective.

Abstract describes dosimetry for mobile PET/SPECT in an ICU; it is not a task-based fMRI study, includes no problem-solving task or task-related brain activation, and involves no relevant healthy adult fMRI sample.

This is an arterial spin labeling (ASL) perfusion MRI study comparing FTD, AD, and controls; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving tasks, and does not report task-related activations in healthy adults.
Materials-science study about conductive polymer sensing skins with no human participants and no fMRI data. It is not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults.

This study is a methodological PET/Monte Carlo simulation paper, not an fMRI task-based study nor involving human participants performing problem-solving tasks. It does not report task-related brain activation in healthy adults.

This study is a PET imaging investigation of tumor texture (18F-FDG PET) in cancer patients, not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It does not involve fMRI, task-related brain activation for problem solving, or healthy adult participants.

Clinical case series of four patients with posterior cortical atrophy describing neuropsychological findings and SPECT/PET imaging. Not a task-based fMRI study and includes no healthy adult sample. Does not report task-related fMRI results relevant to problem-solving in healthy adults.

This is a clinical nephrology study about peritoneal dialysis PET and mathematical modeling of dwell-time creatinine ratios; it is not an fMRI study, does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks, and reports no brain activation results. Exclude based on non-fMRI study and no healthy adult sample.

The study is a radiotherapy planning/clinical study for pharyngo-laryngeal cancer, not a task-based fMRI investigation of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a non-fMRI clinical cancer study and does not include healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks.
This is a PET/CT dosimetry study in patients with neuroendocrine tumours, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving and it does not include a healthy adult sample.

This study is a PET tracer evaluation in patients with brain tumours (nuclear medicine imaging), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves clinical patients and non-fMRI methodology, so it fails key inclusion criteria.

Study uses task-based fMRI of emotional face processing in elderly suicide attempters, not a problem-solving/reasoning task, and does not report a healthy adult (age 18–65) sample. Therefore it does not meet the review population or task criteria.

Abstract reads like a review/discussion rather than reporting a task-based fMRI experiment; participants are adolescents with alcohol use disorders (no healthy adult sample). Therefore it does not meet inclusion criteria.

This study is a PET imaging investigation in non-small cell lung cancer (patients), not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It does not involve healthy adult participants performing reasoning tasks and is a non-fMRI modality, so it meets exclusion criteria.

This study is not a task-based fMRI investigation: it uses FDG-PET-CT in lung cancer patients undergoing radiotherapy. Participants are patients (not healthy adults) and no fMRI task-related brain activation is reported.

Study is an animal (rat) fMRI experiment using electrical forepaw stimulation; it does not include human participants or healthy adults and is not a problem-solving task, so it fails the healthy adult sample criterion and is irrelevant to the review.

This is a task-based fMRI study where human musicians performed mental transposition and melody-reversal tasks; it reports task-related IPS BOLD activation. Although the tasks are auditory transformations rather than classic problem-solving, they involve cognitive manipulation/reasoning and likely used healthy adult participants (musicians). Include for full-text review to confirm participant ages and task fit.

Task-based fMRI study reporting task-related brain activations during moral decision-making (a reasoning/decision task) comparing cultural groups. Abstract does not state participant ages/health explicitly, so include for full-text review to confirm at least one healthy adult group.

This is an fMRI methods study using graded visual stimulation to measure vascular/CBF responses, not a problem-solving or reasoning task in healthy adults. It does not meet the review’s required task (I2) and therefore is not relevant to the meta-analysis objective.

Abstract describes task-based fMRI (mentions motor vs. calculation and reading tasks) but focuses on disorder classification; it does not clearly state participants are healthy adults or that tasks are problem-solving/reasoning, nor does it report activation results. Insufficient information to confirm I2, I3, and I4, so include for full-text review.

The study used resting-state functional MRI to assess connectivity related to golf swing and did not involve a task-based problem-solving or reasoning task. It is a resting-state/connectivity-only study, meeting exclusion criterion E2.

Study uses high-resolution fMRI in rats (non-human) with a connectivity-focused method and sensory stimulation, not a problem-solving task in healthy adult humans. Does not meet population or task requirements.

This is a PET (15O-water) activation study, not an fMRI study, so it fails the non-fMRI exclusion (E4). The task is covert word generation (language), not a problem-solving task. Abstract clearly indicates non-fMRI modality, so exclusion is certain from abstract alone.

Abstract describes examination of intersections of prior fMRI activations and a review of neuropsychological evidence; it appears to be a review/synthesis rather than a primary task-based fMRI experiment in healthy adults. Therefore it meets the exclusion criterion for reviews/meta-analyses.

The study is about microbeam radiation therapy dose calculation and Monte Carlo simulations; it is not an fMRI study and does not involve healthy adult human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Therefore it does not meet the review criteria.
The study is an auditory perception fMRI experiment (sound-level encoding while listening to tones) and does not involve a problem-solving or reasoning task, so it fails inclusion criterion I2.

The study uses a sample of children (no healthy adults aged 18–65), so it does not meet the review’s requirement for adult participants. Other methods and task details are irrelevant given the lack of an adult sample.

This is a resting-state fMRI connectivity study focused on global signal regression and network correlations; it does not report task-based problem-solving or reasoning in healthy adults. Meets exclusion for resting-state/connectivity-only work.
Participants are children in 2nd–3rd grade (ages 7–9); no healthy adult (18–65) sample is included, so the study does not meet the adult-sample inclusion criterion for the meta-analysis.

The study describes Monte Carlo dose calculation for microbeam radiation therapy in preclinical/animal and dosimetry contexts, not task-based fMRI. It does not involve healthy adult human participants or report brain activation during problem-solving.

The study used PET ((18)F-FDG and H2(15)O) to measure glucose metabolism and CBF during a mental calculation task rather than task-based fMRI. The review requires task-based fMRI studies, so this is a non-fMRI study and should be excluded.

The study is about nuclear reactions and isotope production (copper-61) using nuclear model codes—no task-based fMRI, no human participants, and no problem-solving or brain activation data. It clearly does not meet the fMRI/problem-solving inclusion criteria.

Abstract describes a neuroimaging study of a non-verbal matrix reasoning (problem-solving) task and reports cortical activation patterns; however, it does not explicitly state fMRI or participant ages. Include for full-text review to confirm fMRI modality and healthy adult sample.

This article is a review/synthesis of neurocognitive and neuroimaging studies rather than an original task-based fMRI study with participants performing problem-solving tasks; therefore it meets exclusion criterion E1.
The study investigates visual perception suppression using TMS and fMRI (V2/V3) — it is not a problem-solving or reasoning task. It therefore does not meet the review objective (I2).

This is a task-based fMRI study reporting task-related activations (self-construal priming contrasted with a calculation task). The abstract does not clearly state a problem-solving/reasoning task or provide age/health details, so include for full-text review to verify I2 and I3.

Task-based fMRI study reporting task-related activation for arithmetic (problem solving), language, and working memory tasks in human participants. Abstract reports specific brain activations (PMv, POd) and experimental tasks, but does not specify participant ages; full text needed to confirm age range. Include for full-text review.

This is a PET imaging study in juvenile pigs assessing α7 nicotinic receptors, not a task-based fMRI study in human adults performing problem-solving tasks. Excluded for non-fMRI modality and no healthy adult sample.

Preclinical PET study in an animal tumor model; not a task-based fMRI study and includes no healthy adult human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Does not meet inclusion criteria for fMRI, human healthy adults, or problem-solving tasks.
This is a PET/CT methods study on attenuation correction and semi-quantitative FDG PET imaging (phantoms and patients). It is not an fMRI study, nor does it involve problem-solving in healthy adults.
This paper is a methodological/review reflection on 13C-MRS brain metabolism studies, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is non-fMRI work and appears to be a review.

This is a PET (18F-FDG) imaging methods study focused on SUV measurement and reproducibility, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve problem-solving tasks or healthy adult participants (reports lesions/clinical subjects). Therefore it meets exclusion criteria E4 (Non-fMRI) and E3 (No healthy adult sample).

This is an engineering/physics study describing a PET detector prototype, not a task-based fMRI study and it does not involve human participants performing problem-solving tasks. It therefore fails the review objective and meets exclusion criteria.

This is a PET imaging study of tumor proliferation (18F-FLT vs 18F-FDG) in lung nodules/patients—not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks. Excluded for being a non-fMRI study and for having no healthy adult sample.

This is an engineering evaluation of a PET detector using radioactive sources and phantoms; it is not an fMRI study and includes no human participants or problem-solving tasks. Does not meet the review’s inclusion criteria.

This study examines PET/CT metabolic biomarkers in oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma patients—not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. Participants are cancer patients (not healthy adults), and the imaging modality is 18F-FDG PET/CT (not fMRI). Therefore it does not meet the review inclusion criteria.

This is a survey study of pet owners about veterinarian communication and satisfaction; it does not involve any neuroimaging or task-based fMRI, nor do participants perform problem-solving or reasoning tasks. It therefore does not meet the review’s imaging/task requirements.

This is a narrative review about imaging surveillance in rectal cancer (not an original task-based fMRI study). It does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks and is not an fMRI study. Excluded on multiple clear grounds.

This is an FDG-PET study of visual cognition in Parkinson disease patients, not a task-based fMRI study, and the abstract does not describe healthy adult participants or problem-solving tasks. Excluded on non-fMRI modality and lack of healthy adult sample.

This study is a radionuclide therapy / SPECT-PET treatment planning paper for metastatic liver cancer patients, not a task-based fMRI study. It involves patient clinical population (cancer patients), not healthy adults, and does not report task-related brain activation. Exclude.

This is a radiology review/update about renal cell carcinoma imaging (CT, MRI, US), not a task-based fMRI study. It is a review article focused on patient imaging, not healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks, and does not report task-related fMRI activations (E1, E3, E4).
Abstract describes a radiotherapy Monte Carlo treatment-planning model, not an fMRI study. No human participants or problem-solving task; therefore not relevant to task-based fMRI in healthy adults.

This is a PET kinetic modelling study (18F-FDG) comparing iterative parameter estimation methods and compartment models; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not report task-related brain activation or problem-solving tasks, and does not describe a healthy adult task sample.
This is an animal (mouse) PET autoradiography study using 18F-FDG and anesthesia; it is not an fMRI study and does not include healthy adult human participants.

This is a technical PET imaging methods paper using a small-animal scanner (YAP-(S)PET II). It is not an fMRI study and involves no healthy adult human participants; it does not report task-related brain activation. Therefore it is not relevant to a task-based fMRI problem-solving review.
The study is about 18F-FDG PET/CT imaging of atherosclerotic plaque inflammation, not task-based fMRI or problem-solving in healthy adults. It is therefore a non-fMRI study outside the review scope.

Task-based event-related fMRI study where participants performed a numerical problem-solving task (number bisection). The abstract reports task-related activation in parietal and prefrontal regions. Participant health/age not explicitly stated in abstract, so include for full-text confirmation of healthy adult sample.

Abstract reports simultaneous EEG-fMRI during a mental calculation task (a problem-solving task) and describes task-related BOLD (negative and positive) and connectivity changes. Participant health/age not reported in abstract, so full-text is needed to confirm healthy adult sample.

Abstract is a theoretical/review-style discussion of hemispheric specialization and split-brain phenomena; it does not report a task-based fMRI experiment, healthy adult participants, or problem-solving task data. Meets exclusion for being a review/non-empirical article and lacking a healthy adult sample.

The abstract describes a methodological/calibration fMRI study about parameter M and echo time scaling, with no mention of a task-based problem-solving or reasoning paradigm, participant sample, or task-related activation. It does not address the review objective (task-based problem solving in healthy adults).

Abstract clearly reports a task-based fMRI study and task-related activation results, but it does not clearly describe a problem-solving/reasoning task (counting backwards is used as a distractor) nor state participant age/health. Insufficient information to determine whether I2 and I3 are satisfied — include for full-text review.

This is not an fMRI study. It uses N-13 ammonia PET to measure myocardial blood flow during mental stress (cardiac physiology), not task-based brain fMRI or problem-solving tasks. Therefore it meets exclusion E4 (Non-fMRI study).
This study is a PET imaging study of dopamine D2 receptor ligands, not an fMRI task-based study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not involve task-based fMRI or problem-solving tasks.

This study is PET methodology research applied to patients, not task-based fMRI. It does not involve problem-solving tasks or healthy adult fMRI samples. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria for non-fMRI studies and lack of healthy adult sample.

This is a physiological study of ventilatory and cerebral blood flow responses to CO2 using rebreathing methods, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving; no task-related brain activation reported.
The study is a nutritional analysis of plant, algae and insect amino acid content for pet foods — not an fMRI study and does not include human adult participants performing problem-solving tasks.

This is a methods paper about PET scatter simulation and GPU acceleration, not an fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve task-based fMRI, human problem-solving tasks, or report task-related brain activations in healthy adults.

This is a PET study (FDOPA PET) of dopamine storage and aging, not a task-based fMRI investigation of problem solving. It does not involve fMRI methods or problem-solving tasks, so it fails the fMRI requirement.

This study used SPECT with 123I-iodoamphetamine to quantify cerebral blood flow, not fMRI. It does not report task-based fMRI or problem-solving tasks. Therefore it does not meet the fMRI requirement.

Abstract describes a review of executive control development and training, not an original task-based fMRI experiment. It is explicitly a review article, so it meets exclusion criterion E1 (review/meta-analysis).

This abstract is a theoretical/review-style paper proposing a generalized task-related network rather than reporting an original task-based fMRI experiment in healthy adults; it does not present new task-based activation results. Meets exclusion criterion E1 (review/meta-analysis).

This is a task-based fMRI study in which healthy adult grandmaster/master and novice participants performed a Chinese chess problem-solving task; task-evoked network modulations (DMN deactivation) and resting-state connectivity are reported. Thus it meets I1–I4.

Participants are children aged 10–12 with no adult sample reported; therefore the study does not include healthy adults (E3). Although it is a task-based fMRI study of arithmetic problem solving that reports activation, the absence of an adult group excludes it from this review.

This study reports diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) tractography for neurosurgical planning, not task-based fMRI or problem-solving tasks. It is a non-fMRI neuroimaging methods/application paper, so it does not meet the fMRI/problem-solving inclusion criteria.

The study investigates cognitive/dopaminergic dysfunction in Parkinson’s disease; the abstract does not indicate healthy adult participants or that subjects performed a problem-solving task. It therefore does not meet the requirement for a healthy adult problem-solving fMRI study.

This is an engineering study on image-compression algorithms using neural networks; it is not an fMRI study and involves no human participants or task-based problem-solving, so it does not meet the review scope.

The abstract describes a PET scanner hardware/methods study (normalization and detector efficiencies); it is not a task-based fMRI study, includes no human problem-solving task or task-related brain activation. Exclude as a non-fMRI study.

The study is a clinical PET imaging investigation of postoperative spinal infection in patients, not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It does not include healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks and is a non-fMRI modality.

This is a case report of conventional nuclear imaging for a pulmonary nodule (not fMRI), involving a patient with pulmonary disease rather than healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks; no task-based brain activation reported.

This is a veterinary cross-sectional study of 2000 dogs on cataracts. It is not a human task-based fMRI study, includes no healthy adult human participants, and reports no task-related brain activation.

This study describes a CT image quality measurement technique with phantoms; it is not an fMRI study, does not involve human participants performing problem-solving tasks, and does not report task-related brain activation. Therefore it does not meet the review inclusion criteria.

This is a methods paper on image registration using CT/PET/MRI data (patient studies), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report task-related brain activation or involve cognitive problem-solving tasks.

This study is a PET tracer radiochemistry/pharmacokinetics and dosimetry study in healthy men, not a task-based fMRI investigation and does not involve problem-solving or reasoning tasks. It is therefore a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.
Animal study using PET-CT in rats, not a task-based fMRI study of human adults performing problem-solving; does not include healthy adult human participants and is a non-fMRI modality.

Study uses PET to measure myocardial glucose uptake in patients with ischemic cardiomyopathy; it is not an fMRI study and involves a clinical patient sample rather than healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks.

This is a pediatric clinical nephrology study of peritoneal dialysis, not a task-based fMRI study. Participants are children on dialysis (not healthy adults), and no task-related brain activation or fMRI methods are reported.

This study investigates olfaction-based problem solving in pet dogs and reports behavioral results, not task-based fMRI in humans. It therefore lacks healthy adult human participants and is a non-fMRI study.

This study reports 18F-FDG PET imaging for staging lung cancer in patient populations — it is not an fMRI study and does not involve healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks. Excluded for non-fMRI modality and lack of healthy adult sample.

This study is an 18F-FDG PET investigation of CADASIL patients (clinical patient sample), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It therefore uses a non-fMRI modality and does not include a healthy adult task-based problem-solving sample.

Single-case clinical study of a patient with Fahr's disease using MRI and PET; not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants. Does not meet criteria for fMRI task studies in healthy adults.

This study evaluates radiation dosimetry in paediatric models using positron-emitting tracers; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not include healthy adult participants, and is not an fMRI study.

This study is a chemistry/fluorescent probe development and cell imaging paper, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves no fMRI, no problem-solving tasks, and no healthy adult human sample relevant to the review.
Materials science study on CuO nanowire synthesis and devices; no human participants or fMRI data. Does not involve task-based fMRI or problem-solving in healthy adults.

This is a PET/CT image reconstruction methods paper (list-mode reconstruction, motion correction) rather than a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report fMRI task-related activation or problem-solving tasks, so it is not eligible.

This study reports PET imaging with [11C]-WAY-100635 and a method (Hypotime) for mapping 5HT1A receptors; it is not a task-based fMRI study nor does it involve problem-solving tasks in healthy adults. Exclude as non-fMRI.

This is a veterinary review article on regulatory guidelines for ectoparasiticides in animals. It is a review (E1), does not involve human healthy adult participants (E3), and is not an fMRI study (E4).

The study investigates hippocampal serotonin metabolism and 5-HT1A receptor density in patients with amnestic MCI and mild AD; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving tasks, and includes no healthy adult sample.

This is a resting-state fMRI methods/connectivity study focused on accelerating voxel-wise connectivity computation; it does not involve a task-based problem-solving or reasoning paradigm in healthy adults and reports connectivity methods rather than task-related activations.

This is a diagnostic PET study in transplant recipients with suspected posttransplant lymphoproliferative disorder. It is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks, and is not an fMRI study.

This is an animal PET biodistribution/metabolism study in normal dogs, not a task-based fMRI study in human adults. It does not involve human participants performing problem-solving tasks or report task-related fMRI activations.
Animal cardiac PET study in mice measuring myocardial glucose uptake with 18F-FDG; not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult human participants.

This study is a PET kinetic modeling study of a radioligand (11C-SB207145) for 5-HT4 receptors in humans, not a task-based fMRI investigation of problem-solving. It does not involve fMRI or task-related brain activation for problem solving, so it fails the fMRI requirement.

This is a methodological guideline/review (RECIST 1.1) on tumor imaging criteria, not an original task-based fMRI study. It does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks and is not an fMRI activation study. Therefore it meets multiple exclusion criteria.

This is a behavioral neuropsychological study on frontal aphasic patients (with matched controls) using an action-comprehension picture-ordering task; the abstract does not report any task-based fMRI results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the fMRI requirement.

This is a narrative review about imaging of radiation necrosis, not an original task-based fMRI study. It does not involve healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks nor report task-related fMRI activation results.

Abstract describes physiological measures of cerebral blood flow/volume and oxygen metabolism with age; it does not mention task-based fMRI, problem-solving tasks, or task-related activation. Appears to be a non-fMRI/physiology aging study.
This is a PET methods paper focused on global flow estimation, not an fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a non-fMRI study, so exclusion criterion E4 applies.

This study investigated diffusion MRI (ADC) in head and neck cancer patients to predict chemoradiation outcome. It is not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving and does not include healthy adult participants; therefore it meets exclusion criteria for non-fMRI methods and no healthy adult sample.

This is a clinical management paper on spinal metastases of lung cancer, not a task-based fMRI study. It concerns patients with a medical condition (not healthy adult participants) and does not report task-related brain activation. Therefore it does not meet the review objectives.

The abstract discusses radiotherapy target delineation and imaging methods, not task-based fMRI or problem-solving in healthy adults. It appears to be a review in radiation oncology and does not report task-related brain activation in healthy adults.
This is a radiochemistry methods paper describing synthesis of a PET radioligand; it does not report any task-based fMRI in human participants or healthy adult samples.
This is a radiopharmaceutical dosimetry/modeling study, not a task-based fMRI investigation. It does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks and uses non-fMRI methods.

The abstract describes a technical dynamic MR perfusion study and correction algorithm for arterial input function saturation; it is not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study.

This is a PET dosimetry study using a bladder phantom, not a task-based fMRI study; it does not involve human healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks. Excluded for being a non-fMRI study and for lacking a healthy adult sample.

This is a PET image reconstruction methods paper (list-mode MLEM and ML position estimation), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report fMRI task-related brain activation in humans.

This is a non-human (dog) behavioral study conducted in a veterinary/clinical setting and does not use fMRI. It therefore lacks any healthy adult human participants and is not an fMRI study, meeting exclusion criteria E3 and E4.

Preclinical PET imaging study in rats assessing cardiac function; not a task-based fMRI study, contains no human healthy adult participants, and does not involve problem-solving tasks. Exclude for non-fMRI and no healthy adult sample.

This is a task-based fMRI study in healthy participants performing spatial judgement/mental imagery (Mental Clock) decisions (reasoning-like task). The abstract reports task-related activation contrasts and hemispheric effects, meeting inclusion criteria.

The study applies a connectivity-focused method to a simple motor task (unilateral hand movements), not a problem-solving or reasoning task. Although task-based fMRI and some task-related activation are reported, it does not investigate problem solving in healthy adults, so it is not relevant to the review objective.

This is a federal Medicare policy/final rule, not an empirical task-based fMRI study. It does not involve human participants performing problem-solving tasks nor report task-related brain activation or fMRI data, so it fails core inclusion criteria.

This is a PET imaging study of hepatocellular carcinoma using 18F-FLT in clinical (cancer) patients. It is not an fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving tasks, and does not include healthy adult participants.

This is a task-based fMRI study of syntactic/language processing (jabberwocky sentences), not a problem-solving or reasoning task, so it fails inclusion criterion I2. It reports task activations but does not investigate problem solving in healthy adults. Exclusion is based on lack of relevant task domain.
The study uses affect-processing tasks and resting-state fMRI; it does not involve a problem-solving or reasoning task, so inclusion criterion I2 is not met. It does meet I1, I3, and I4.

This is an animal (mouse) modeling study of hematopoiesis and radiation toxicity, not a task-based fMRI study and it includes no healthy adult human participants. Fails inclusion criteria and clearly meets exclusion criteria E3 and E4.

This is a preclinical engineering paper describing a small-animal radiation device (MicroRT). It involves animal experiments and radiation hardware, not human task-based fMRI or problem-solving tasks. Excluded for no healthy adult sample and non-fMRI methodology.

This study uses PET with a radioligand ([18F]MPPF) to measure 5-HT1A receptor binding in humans; it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not involve problem-solving or reasoning tasks in healthy adults. Therefore it does not meet the fMRI/task-related inclusion criteria.

This is a preclinical imaging study in rats using a radiotracer (PET), not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not include healthy adult participants and is not an fMRI study, so it fails the review criteria.
This is a veterinary study measuring fecal IgA in dogs (animal subjects), not a task-based human fMRI study. It does not involve healthy adult human participants and is not an fMRI study.

This study reports tumour incidence in insured dogs (non-human subjects) and is not an fMRI task study. It therefore lacks a healthy adult human sample and is not a task-based fMRI study, so it does not meet the review inclusion criteria.

Abstract describes an experiment on neurobiological influence of expert advice on financial decisions but does not specify task-based fMRI, participant ages/health, or whether the task qualifies as problem-solving. Insufficient information to determine eligibility from abstract alone; include for full-text review.

The abstract describes a task-based fMRI study and reports task-related brain activation, but it does not mention any problem-solving or reasoning task or clearly state sample age/health. Unclear whether participants performed a problem-solving task, so include for full-text review.

Study uses median nerve sensory stimulation and investigates intrinsic connectivity/metabolic coupling, not a problem-solving or reasoning task; thus it does not meet the review's requirement for a problem-solving task.

This study evaluates CT-based lung mass estimation for internal dosimetry, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve fMRI, task-related brain activation, or problem-solving tasks in healthy adults. Excluded as a non-fMRI study.
This is a resting-state fMRI study (ALFF) in medication-naïve ADHD boys (children), not a task-based problem-solving study in healthy adults. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria E2 and E3.

This is a computational/medical-physics study about dose kernels for radionuclide therapy, not a task-based fMRI study and does not involve human participants performing problem-solving tasks. It does not meet the review topic.

This is a methodological guideline/report on radiopharmaceutical biodistribution and dosimetry, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a non-fMRI review/guidance document.

This is a review article summarizing tracer studies of serotonin synthesis (alpha-MTrp), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review (E1) and concerns non-fMRI methods (E4).

This study is a PET tracer biodistribution/dosimetry study using 18F-FDG labelled leukocytes in healthy volunteers; it is not a task-based fMRI study nor does it involve problem-solving or report task-related brain activation. Therefore it does not meet the review objective.

The study describes a radionuclide calibrator method for short-lived radioactive sources; it is not an fMRI study, contains no human participants performing problem-solving tasks, and reports no task-related brain activation.

This is an engineering paper on fan-beam image reconstruction (CT algorithm), not a task-based fMRI study; it includes no human participants or problem-solving task and reports algorithmic/technical results rather than task-related brain activation.

This is a PET ([18F]-FDG) nuclear medicine study of tracer uptake in atherosclerotic plaque and renal function, not a task-based fMRI study nor involving problem-solving or reasoning tasks in healthy adults. It does not meet the fMRI/problem-solving inclusion criteria.

The abstract describes a clinical treatment study/review of selective internal radiation therapy in patients with liver tumours—not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. Participants are patients, not healthy adults, and no fMRI data are reported.

This is a veterinary clinical study of canine pyoderma focusing on prescribing practices and prevalence in dogs. It is not an fMRI study and does not include human healthy adult participants; therefore it fails inclusion and meets exclusion criteria.
Study is about ion beam treatment planning and experimental cell culture, not task-based fMRI or human problem-solving. It is a non-fMRI, non-human study.

This is an engineering study of PET detector hardware (laser-processed crystal blocks and SiPM readout). It is not an fMRI study and includes no human participants or healthy adult sample. Therefore it does not meet the review criteria.

This study concerns a mathematical model for pediatric peritoneal dialysis and is not a task-based fMRI study; it also does not include healthy adult participants. Therefore it does not meet the review criteria.

This is an 18F-FDG PET study of cervical cancer patients (clinical oncology), not a task-based fMRI study; participants are patients (not healthy adults) and no problem-solving task or task-related fMRI activation is reported.

This is a non-systematic review about cognition in schizophrenia and antipsychotics, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves patient populations and is a review, and does not report task-related fMRI results.

This is a PET imaging study (FLB 457) measuring D2 receptor density, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve problem-solving tasks or report task-related fMRI activation. Exclusion criterion E4 applies.

This study investigates SUV methodology in oesophageal cancer (PET imaging) and does not involve task-based fMRI, problem-solving tasks, or a healthy adult sample. Therefore it does not meet the review objective.

This is an environmental/economic case study of water kiosks, not a task-based fMRI study and contains no brain imaging, cognitive problem-solving tasks, or healthy adult fMRI data. Excluded as a non-fMRI study.
The study involves dogs and veterinary biomarkers, not human participants or task-based fMRI. It does not include healthy adult humans (E3) and is not an fMRI study (E4).

This is a methods paper on PET parametric image reconstruction and kinetic model assessment; it is not a task-based fMRI study, contains no problem-solving task or healthy adult fMRI data. Not relevant to the review objective.

This is a microbiology study on bacterial VNTR loci for Staphylococcus aureus typing; it is not a task-based fMRI study nor does it include human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Does not meet inclusion criteria I1–I4.

The abstract describes radiometal production and cyclotron target methods (Sc-44 production), not a task-based fMRI study. It involves no human participants performing problem-solving tasks and is a non-fMRI, technical/nuclear medicine methods paper.

Study examines cerebral blood flow in patients with acute intracerebral hemorrhage using pharmacologic blood pressure reductions; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks, and reports no fMRI activation results. Exclude for E3 and E4.

The study describes development/evaluation of patient-specific internal dosimetry in nuclear medicine—not a task-based fMRI study, contains no problem-solving tasks or healthy adult behavioral sample. Exclude as non-fMRI and no healthy adult sample.
This is a PET imaging study using [11C]MADAM to quantify serotonin transporter availability, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not meet the fMRI requirement.

This is an animal/pet-food chemistry study measuring gross energy and fibre; it does not involve human participants, task-based fMRI, or report brain activation. Excluded due to non-fMRI study and absence of healthy adult sample.

Study is an oncology radiotherapy planning study using IMRT for lung cancer patients, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. Participants are patients with locally advanced non-small-cell lung cancer (not healthy adults). Does not report task-related brain activation. Exclusion criteria E4 and E3 apply.

This study is a dosimetric analysis of 177Lu-DOTATATE treatment in patients with neuroendocrine tumors. It is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving or reasoning tasks, and does not include healthy adult participants.

This is an animal study of canine feeding practices and weight loss, not a task-based fMRI study in human adults. It does not include healthy adult human participants and is not an fMRI study, so it fails inclusion criteria. Exclusions E3 and E4 apply.

This is an epidemiological seroprevalence survey of toxoplasmosis in pregnant women; it does not report any task-based fMRI, problem-solving tasks, or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is not an fMRI study and does not meet the review objectives.

This is a pathology/prognostic study of pancreatic endocrine tumors in patients, not a task-based fMRI investigation of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not involve fMRI or healthy adult participants.

Abstract is a general review/opinion about education and technology with no report of task-based fMRI methods, no participant data, and no healthy adult sample studied. It does not present original fMRI task activation results.

This is an RCT of action-observation therapy in children with hemiplegic cerebral palsy, not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. Participants are pediatric patients (not healthy adults) and the study is non-fMRI, so it clearly fails inclusion criteria.

This is an epidemiological study of soil-transmitted helminth infections in a tribal population, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report any fMRI task-related brain activation or involve problem-solving tasks.
This is a biochemical/structural study of a protein-DNA system in Drosophila, not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It contains no human participants or fMRI data.

The study concerns dosimetry of radiotracers in nuclear medicine diagnostics, not task-based fMRI or problem-solving in humans. It does not involve fMRI methods or report task-related brain activation in healthy adults.
This study is a PET FDG metabolism study in tumors (oncology), not a task-based fMRI investigation; it does not involve problem-solving tasks or healthy adult participants.

The study is about MIBG imaging and therapy in medullary thyroid carcinoma patients (clinical/nuclear medicine), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves patient populations rather than healthy adults and does not report task-related fMRI activations.

This study is a PET radioligand imaging study quantifying [18F]MPPF binding to 5-HT1A receptors, not a task-based fMRI study and it does not involve participants performing a problem-solving/reasoning task. Therefore it does not meet the review objective.
Study is an animal nutrition study of lorikeets (birds), not a task-based fMRI investigation in humans. It does not involve human participants or fMRI; therefore it does not meet inclusion criteria.

This is a PET myocardial perfusion imaging methods/validation study of cardiac software, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults; no task-related brain activation results are reported.

This is a PET/SPET simulation and ligand-binding study, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not involve fMRI, task-based activation, or problem-solving tasks, so it meets exclusion criterion E4 (non-fMRI).

This is a nuclear medicine radioimmunotherapy dosimetry study (SPECT/CT and Monte Carlo modeling) in tumor patients, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a non-fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants.

This study describes Monte Carlo simulations for dose point kernels in nuclear medicine; it is not an fMRI study, includes no human problem-solving tasks or healthy adult participants. Exclude due to non-fMRI methods and no healthy adult sample.

Study is a PET radiotracer pharmacokinetics investigation in patients with metastatic neuroendocrine tumours, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It includes patient sample (no healthy adults) and is non-fMRI.
Animal SPECT tracer study in mice; not an fMRI study and does not include healthy adult human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Does not meet review objective.

This study is a radiobiologic modeling paper on tumor repopulation and radiotherapy planning, not a task-based fMRI study. It involves no human participants performing problem-solving tasks and does not report fMRI activation results.

This is an evidence-based diagnostic review/report of stress echocardiography in patients suspected of coronary artery disease — not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a non-fMRI review and involves patient samples, not healthy adult task-based fMRI.

The abstract describes a review of Cerenkov luminescence imaging (an optical/radioisotope imaging modality), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a non-fMRI review article, so it fails the review objective.

This is a review article summarizing PET and MRI activation studies of tremor rather than an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It therefore meets the exclusion criterion for reviews/meta-analyses (E1).

This is a narrative/review article summarizing neuroimaging techniques in neuropsychiatry rather than a primary task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report task-related activation or an experimental problem-solving task.

This is a narrative review of FDG-PET/PET-CT in pediatric oncology, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves pediatric cancer patients and imaging modalities other than fMRI.
Abstract is a review article summarizing development and applications of PET and fMRI rather than reporting an original task-based fMRI study in healthy adults. Excluded as a review (E1).

This is a 2013 narrative review of PET imaging for inflammation biomarkers, not an original task-based fMRI study. It does not involve problem-solving tasks in healthy adults and uses PET rather than fMRI. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria.
This is a review of PET-CT in oncology (not a task-based fMRI study), concerns clinical oncology patients rather than healthy adults, and is therefore a non-fMRI review article.

This is not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It discusses PET imaging in patients with multiple system atrophy and appears to be a review of PET findings; no task-based fMRI, no healthy adult sample, and likely a review article.

This is a review article about fMRI in epilepsy (not an original task-based fMRI study). It does not report task-based problem-solving or reasoning results in healthy adults. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria E1 (review) and E3 (no healthy adult sample).
This is a review of PET imaging in lung cancer (non-fMRI), focusing on patients/tumors rather than healthy adult problem-solving. It is a review and non-fMRI study, and lacks a healthy adult sample.

This is explicitly a review article discussing hybrid imaging modalities (PET/CT, SPECT/CT, PET/MR) in oncology rather than a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It is non-fMRI and a review, so it does not meet the review’s focus.

The abstract describes a review of cardiac positron emission tomography (PET), not an fMRI study or task-based problem-solving research in healthy adults. It appears to be a review article focused on PET imaging of the heart, so it does not meet the review’s fMRI/problem-solving criteria.

This is a narrative review of integrated whole-body MR/PET systems and clinical applications, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review article and concerns MR/PET (not fMRI task activation).

This article is a narrative review of fetal functional imaging rather than an original task-based fMRI study in adults. It does not report primary task-related fMRI results in healthy adult participants.

This study uses PET to assess skeletal muscle glucose metabolism, not task-based fMRI of problem-solving. It examines metabolic imaging in muscle, not cognitive tasks in healthy adults, so it does not meet the review objective.

Study uses PET (not fMRI), is an overview/review-style paper rather than a task-based experiment, and does not report task-based problem-solving in healthy adult participants. Therefore it does not meet the review objective.

The abstract describes cardiac PET imaging (non-fMRI) and reads like a review of myocardial perfusion PET for coronary disease. It does not involve task-based fMRI, problem-solving tasks, or healthy adult cognitive fMRI samples. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria E4 (Non-fMRI study) and E1 (Review).

This is a review article (published in Sleep Medicine Reviews) about lucid dreaming and psychosis, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It does not report primary task-related fMRI results for problem solving, so it meets exclusion criterion E1.

This is a review article on PET imaging in newborns (neonatal population), not a task-based fMRI study in healthy adults. It is a review, uses PET (non-fMRI), and does not include healthy adult participants, so it fails the inclusion criteria.

This is a methodological outline/proposal for fMRI investigation of nausea/vomiting, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem-solving. The abstract does not describe a problem-solving or reasoning task, nor report task-related activation results, so it is out of scope for the review.

This is a review discussing PET studies of auditory processing, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It focuses on PET (non-fMRI) methods and auditory tasks, so it does not meet the review’s fMRI or problem-solving criteria.

This abstract describes a review of PET neurochemical imaging in addiction, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review article and uses PET (non-fMRI), so it clearly meets exclusion criteria E1 and E4.
This article is a review of neuroimaging studies in HIV rather than a primary task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults, meeting the review/meta-analysis exclusion criterion.

This is a review of FDG-PET imaging in mild traumatic brain injury, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It is a review article (E1), involves non-fMRI molecular imaging (E4), and does not include healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks (E3).

This is a methodological review of PET/SPECT for drug receptor occupancy, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review article and does not report task-based fMRI activations.

Abstract describes PET/SPECT/MRI studies in patients with liver cirrhosis and summarizes imaging findings; it does not report task-based fMRI of problem-solving in healthy adults. Excluded because non-fMRI modalities are used (E4).

This is a review about FDG-PET imaging of coronary inflammation, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review (E1), involves non-fMRI imaging modalities (E4), and does not include a healthy adult problem-solving sample (E3).
This is a review article focusing on PET studies in Parkinson’s disease patients (not healthy adults). It is not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants.

Abstract presents a conceptual/review discussion of fMRI of the trigeminal system and opportunities for studying pain; it does not report an original task-based fMRI experiment of problem-solving in healthy adults. Classified as a review article, so excluded (E1).

This is a clinical review article on PET/CT imaging in non-Hodgkin lymphoma, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review, involves patient populations rather than healthy adults, and concerns PET/CT (not fMRI).

This is a review of PET studies in Alzheimer's patients, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves non-fMRI modality (PET), includes AD patients rather than healthy adults, and is a review.

This is a review article about PET imaging in sarcoidosis patients, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review (E1) and involves non-fMRI imaging modalities (PET) rather than fMRI (E4).

Abstract is an overview/review of fMRI applications to the visual system rather than an original task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It summarizes methods and clinical applications, meeting the exclusion criterion for reviews.

This is a review article summarizing multi-modal neuroimaging and genetics rather than an original task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults, so it meets exclusion criterion E1 (review).

This is a review of PET studies in epilepsy rather than an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review (E1), uses PET not fMRI (E4), and does not involve a healthy adult sample performing problem-solving tasks (E3).

This paper is a systematic review of neuroimaging in essential tremor (not an original task-based fMRI study). It does not report task-based fMRI results of healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks. It therefore meets exclusion criterion E1 (review/meta-analysis).

Abstract describes a review of functional neuroimaging methods and applications (SPECT, PET, fMRI) and aging/drug effects, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review article, so excluded per E1.

The study is a cost analysis of PET facilities and does not report task-based fMRI research, nor any healthy adult task-based problem-solving data. It is a non-fMRI, non-task neuroimaging methods/costs paper.
This is a review article on PET molecular imaging (non-[(18)F]FDG tracers) rather than an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is non-fMRI and a review.

Abstract focuses on thalamic activation and working memory in Kleine-Levin syndrome; it does not clearly state a problem-solving/reasoning task or whether healthy adult controls were included. Insufficient information to determine eligibility for a problem-solving fMRI meta-analysis—include for full-text review.

This is a clinical technology review of PET/PET–CT for head and neck cancer (patients), not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It is a review, involves patient populations, and reports non-fMRI imaging methods.
This is a PET methods/overview paper (non-fMRI) and does not report task-based fMRI results or problem-solving tasks in healthy adults. It therefore fails the fMRI requirement.

This is a review article summarizing spinal cord and brainstem fMRI methods and findings, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It meets the exclusion criterion for reviews (E1) and does not address problem-solving tasks in healthy adult participants.

This is a review article about CT/MRI/PET detection of recurrent rectal cancer (not an original task-based fMRI study). It does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks and is not an fMRI experiment. Therefore it meets multiple exclusion criteria.

This is a PET overview/background article (review), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report task-related fMRI activations. Exclusion criteria E1 (review) and E4 (non-fMRI) apply.

This study is not a task-based fMRI study: it describes myocardial perfusion imaging using rubidium-82 PET and appears to be a review. It does not involve problem-solving tasks or report fMRI activation in healthy adults. Exclusion criteria E4 (non-fMRI) and likely E1 (review) apply.

Abstract describes a methodological review of MEG beamforming rather than an original task-based fMRI experiment. It reviews previous MEG studies and is not an fMRI study or primary task activation report.
This is a review article describing the sensorimotor system and fMRI applications, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It meets exclusion criterion E1.

This article is a technical review (not a primary task-based fMRI study) and focuses on aphasic stroke patients rather than healthy adult participants. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria E1 (review) and E3 (no healthy adult sample).
The article is a review examining PET imaging of insulin resistance, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review (E1) and a non-fMRI study (E4).

This is a review article summarizing human PET studies of histamine; it is not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria for being a review (E1) and for being a non-fMRI study (E4).

Abstract describes an overview/comparison of neuroimaging (MSI and fMRI) for olfactory stimulation and reads like a review of methods/results, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not involve problem-solving tasks or report relevant task activations for the review objective.

This is a review article summarizing functional neuroimaging studies of vagus nerve stimulation rather than an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It meets exclusion criterion E1 (review/meta-analysis).

Abstract reports an fMRI study of working memory in schizophrenia to examine insight; it does not indicate inclusion of healthy adult participants or use of a problem-solving/reasoning task. Excluded because no healthy adult sample is apparent from the abstract (E3).

The study uses fMRI to measure brain responses to glucose/fructose infusions and does not involve a problem-solving or reasoning task. It does not address the review objective of task-based fMRI during problem solving in healthy adults.

This is an expert guideline/review on PET/CT imaging in lymphoma (clinical imaging), not an original task-based fMRI study. It involves patients with lymphoma (not healthy adults) and uses PET/CT rather than fMRI, so it meets exclusion criteria E1, E3, and E4.

This study reports FDG-PET/CT imaging of parathyroid carcinoma (clinical cancer patients) and is a literature review with case series — not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is non-fMRI, involves patients, and is a review/case series.

This is a review chapter on PET and SPECT in Parkinson's disease (not an original task-based fMRI study). It uses non-fMRI modalities and does not study healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks. Exclude per review status and non-fMRI methods.

The abstract describes PET/CT nuclear cardiology developments and myocardial perfusion imaging (not fMRI), focuses on instrumentation/techniques rather than task-based neuroimaging or problem-solving tasks, and reads as a review. Thus it is a non-fMRI study and a review, so excluded.

Abstract describes language reorganization in patients with early-onset temporal lobe epilepsy using fMRI. It does not report a problem-solving/reasoning task and does not indicate a healthy adult (18–65) sample. Exclude due to absence of healthy adult participants.

This is a review paper of fMRI studies in schizophrenia patients focusing on antipsychotic effects, not a primary task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It therefore meets exclusion criteria for being a review and for lacking a healthy adult sample.

The study is a task-based fMRI investigation of external source memory/cognitive insight in non-clinical subjects. It does not involve a problem-solving or reasoning task required by the review (I2 not met). No exclusion criteria from E1–E4 clearly apply, but the task domain does not match the meta-analysis objective.

This is an overview/review of hybrid PET/MRI instrumentation (a methods/technology paper), not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It is a review/non-original fMRI study and does not involve task-based fMRI of human problem-solving.
This is an animal (pig) PET imaging study of angiotensin receptors, not a task-based fMRI study in human adults performing problem-solving; thus it is not fMRI and has no healthy adult sample.

The abstract describes a task-based fMRI study that reports task-related brain activations (left inferior frontal and superior temporal cortex). However, the task involves language/semantic integration (words and pictures into sentences), not clearly a problem-solving or reasoning task, and participant age/health status are not specified. Insufficient information to determine whether the task meets the review's problem-solving criterion or whether participants are healthy adults; include for full-text review.

The abstract describes CT/PET imaging for prostate cancer (clinical review of imaging techniques), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It appears to be a review/non-fMRI clinical imaging paper with no healthy adult cognitive tasks reported.

This article is a literature review of imaging studies (MEDLINE search, synthesis of evidence) rather than an original task-based fMRI experiment in healthy adults, so it meets the exclusion criterion for reviews/meta-analyses.

This study uses PET-CT in patients with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, not task-based fMRI, and does not include healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks. It does not meet the review objective.

This study uses PET imaging (PIB and FDDNP) to visualize amyloid and tangles in Alzheimer’s and nondemented elderly subjects; it is not an fMRI study nor a task-based problem-solving study. Exclude due to non-fMRI methodology.

This appears to be a brief commentary/editorial about another fMRI study on perception of grasping, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report a problem-solving or reasoning task and reads like a review/perspective.

The study is a cost-analysis of FDG-PET procedures, not a task-based fMRI investigation. It does not involve participants performing problem-solving/reasoning tasks or report task-related brain activation in healthy adults.

This is a chapter/review of nonhuman primate PET imaging in addiction, not a task-based fMRI study in humans. It involves nonhuman subjects and PET methodology and appears to be a review chapter, so it fails inclusion. Exclude for E3, E4, and E1.

The abstract identifies this paper as a review of simultaneous EEG/fMRI and resting-state networks rather than an original task-based fMRI experiment of problem solving in healthy adults. Thus it meets exclusion criteria for being a review (E1) and focuses on resting-state/connectivity (E2).

The study focuses on functional and anatomical connectivity in schizophrenia using fMRI and DTI (connectivity-focused) rather than task-based problem-solving in healthy adults. It does not report task-based problem-solving in healthy adult participants.

This is a narrative review of Parkinson's disease focused on PET and therapeutic interventions, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It discusses PET (non-fMRI) and disease populations, not healthy adult problem-solving tasks.

This is a review article using PET methods on dopamine in Parkinson’s disease and health, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. Excluded as a review (E1) and non-fMRI modality (E4).

This is a review article of resting-state fMRI studies in Parkinson’s disease (not a task-based fMRI study). It is a review (E1) and focuses on resting-state fMRI literature (E2), not task-based problem-solving in healthy adults.

Abstract explicitly states this is a review of PET imaging principles and technology; it is not an original task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. Therefore it meets the exclusion criterion for reviews (E1).

Abstract describes task-based fMRI in patients with schizophrenia using a social cognition paradigm; it does not mention any healthy adult (18–65) control group and the task is social cognition rather than problem-solving. Thus it does not meet the requirement for a healthy adult problem-solving fMRI study.

The article is a review/critical commentary on fMRI methods and not a primary task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It summarizes methods and discusses connectivity/DTI and media representation rather than reporting task activation data.
The study uses task-based fMRI of colour perception/colour-center mapping, not a problem-solving or reasoning task, so it does not meet inclusion criterion I2 (problem-solving).

Abstract focuses on self-reflection and neural correlates in schizophrenia but does not specify whether a healthy adult control group or a problem-solving task was used. Information is insufficient to determine inclusion based on the review’s strict criteria, so include for full-text review.

The task is lower-limb stepping (motor control), not a problem-solving or reasoning task; thus it fails inclusion criterion I2 despite being a task-based fMRI with healthy adults and reporting activation.

This is an EEG-fMRI case study of a single patient with fixation-off sensitivity; it does not include healthy adult participants and does not involve a problem-solving task. Therefore it fails the requirement for a healthy adult sample and is not relevant to the review objective.

The fMRI task was temporal discrimination (sensory timing), not a problem-solving or reasoning task required by the review objective (does not meet I2). Although this is a task-based fMRI study reporting activation (I1, I4) in participants including healthy controls, the task content excludes it from the meta-analysis.

The study is not an fMRI study; it describes PET imaging of (64)Cu-labeled liposomes in tumor models and patients and focuses on drug delivery. Participants are tumor-bearing xenografts and patients, not healthy adults. Therefore it fails the fMRI requirement and does not include healthy adult samples.

This study used 18F-FDG PET (not fMRI) in patients with anxiety-obsessive disorders; it does not include healthy adult participants nor task-based fMRI problem-solving data. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria E4 and E3.

The study was performed on post-mortem subjects (no healthy adult participants) and does not involve task-based problem-solving; it therefore fails to include a healthy adult sample and does not address the review objective.

The article is a review discussing multimodal PET-fMRI methods and examples, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults, thus it meets the exclusion criterion for reviews/meta-analyses.

Abstract reports MEG+fMRI task-based imaging of somatosensory and auditory spatial processing but does not mention problem-solving or participant ages. Insufficient information to confirm presence of problem-solving tasks or healthy adult sample, so include for full-text review.

This study uses resting-state fMRI and dynamic causal modeling to examine default mode network connectivity, not a task-based problem-solving or reasoning paradigm. It is a resting-state/connectivity-focused study and does not report task-related activations.

Animal study (anesthetized rats) using optical imaging of microvascular oxygen and flow, not task-based fMRI in healthy adult humans performing problem-solving tasks. Does not meet human fMRI or healthy adult criteria.

This is a task-based fMRI study of memory in presymptomatic familial AD carriers and noncarriers, not a problem-solving or reasoning task in healthy adults. The focus is clinical (FAD) rather than healthy adult problem solving, so it does not meet the review objective.

Abstract reports a task-based fMRI study and presents task-related brain activation results, but the task is described as a language task (not clearly a problem-solving/reasoning task) and participant ages/health are not specified. Include for full-text review to confirm whether the task meets the problem-solving criterion and that healthy adults (18-65) were studied.

The study describes PET imaging of P-glycoprotein function and methodology, not a task-based fMRI investigation of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a non-fMRI study, so it does not meet the meta-analysis topic or methods.

This is a methods paper presenting an fMRI vessel-size estimation technique; the abstract does not describe a task-based problem-solving or reasoning paradigm, nor does it report results from healthy adult participants performing such tasks. It is not relevant to the review objective.

This is an animal (rodent) fMRI study stimulating peripheral nerves, not a task-based problem-solving or reasoning study in human adults. It does not include healthy adult human participants, so it fails inclusion criterion I3 (exclude by E3).

Participants are children (previously institutionalized and same-aged comparison), not healthy adults (18–65). The task is an emotional face Go/No-go, not a problem-solving/reasoning task. Therefore the study does not meet the adult healthy sample requirement.

Abstract describes an overview/review of a thesis on neurobiology and genetics of depression, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. No details on a problem-solving task, fMRI results, or healthy adult sample are provided.
Non-human (rat) study; does not include healthy adult human participants or a problem-solving task. Fails inclusion for human healthy adults required by the review.

The study uses task-based fMRI for language/auditory sentence processing and focuses on effective and structural connectivity analyses (GCM and DTPM). It does not investigate problem-solving or reasoning tasks and is a connectivity-focused study, meeting exclusion criterion E2.

Abstract describes an overview/tutorial introducing fMRI methods and exemplary studies in psychiatric (schizophrenia) populations rather than reporting an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. This is a review-type article, meeting exclusion E1.

This study uses resting-state functional connectivity measured with diffuse optical tomography (DOT), not task-based fMRI of problem solving. It is a resting-state/connectivity-focused, non-fMRI modality, so it does not meet the review’s task-based fMRI/problem-solving criteria.

Case report of a sinonasal sarcoma with histopathology and imaging; not an fMRI study, no task-based problem-solving paradigm, and no healthy adult sample. Does not meet the meta-analysis inclusion requirements.

The study investigates perception of dynamic/static emotional facial expressions, not problem-solving or reasoning. Although it uses task-based fMRI/EEG in healthy adults and reports task-related activation, it does not meet inclusion criterion I2 (problem-solving).

The study uses task-based fMRI in hyposmic idiopathic Parkinson's disease patients (no healthy adult group) and examines olfactory processing rather than problem-solving tasks. It therefore lacks a healthy adult sample (exclusion E3) and does not address problem-solving/reasoning.
The study investigates emotion regulation using fMRI, not problem-solving or reasoning tasks; therefore it fails the key inclusion criterion I2 (not a problem-solving task).
Animal PET study of radiolabeled nanoparticles delivered to rat brain; not a human task fMRI study of problem-solving. Does not involve healthy adult human participants and is not fMRI-based.

This study used resting-state fMRI in first-episode MDD patients (no task-based problem-solving and no healthy adult sample). It therefore meets exclusion criteria for resting-state studies and lack of healthy adult participants.

This is a PET pharmacokinetic study of P-glycoprotein using (R)-[11C]verapamil in rats and humans, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve fMRI task activation in healthy adults; thus it meets exclusion criterion E4 (non-fMRI study).

This is a PET (18F-FDG) imaging study of spinal inflammation in patients, not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. Participants are described as patients undergoing clinical PET, so no healthy adult sample is reported. Exclusion criteria E4 and E3 apply.

This is an fMRI study of thermal pain stimulation, not a problem-solving or reasoning task; thus it does not meet inclusion criterion I2. Other inclusion criteria (I1, I3, I4) are met but the key task domain is irrelevant to the review objective.

Abstract describes a task-based fMRI study using movie-clips and behavioral ratings, but it does not clearly indicate a problem-solving or reasoning task. Insufficient information to confirm I2 and I3 from abstract alone — include for full-text review.

This study uses functional neuroimaging to investigate startle-induced seizures in patients with startle epilepsy and does not involve healthy adults performing problem-solving or reasoning tasks. It therefore does not meet the review's participant/task criteria.
The study uses resting-state fMRI (rs-fMRI) for preoperative mapping rather than a task-based fMRI paradigm involving problem solving in healthy adults, meeting exclusion criterion E2.

This article is a narrative/review discussing PET and fMRI methods and applications, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report task activation results from a sample, so it meets the review/meta-analysis exclusion criterion for reviews.

The fMRI task is product evaluation following time vs money primes, not a problem-solving or reasoning task; thus it does not meet inclusion criterion I2. It does appear to be a task-based fMRI study reporting task-related activation (insula).

Study examines pharmacological effects of sibutramine in obese women (clinical sample) under fasting; participants are not healthy adults and task is not problem-solving. Does not meet the review population/ task requirements.

The study uses task-based fMRI of motor and sensory tasks related to musical training, not problem-solving or reasoning tasks. Therefore it does not meet the review’s requirement for a problem-solving task (I2).

Study reports PET in eight patients with frontotemporal dementia; it is not an fMRI study and includes no healthy adult participants, so it does not meet the review’s fMRI and healthy adult sample requirements.

This is a clinical case report of inflammatory breast cancer with surgical/medical management; it is not an fMRI study, does not include healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks, and reports no task-related brain activation.

The abstract describes PET imaging in oncology and appears to be an overview/review of PET applications. It is not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria for being a review (E1) and a non-fMRI study (E4).
Study uses task-based fMRI of object-based attention (shifting between faces and houses) rather than a problem-solving or reasoning task, so it does not meet inclusion criterion I2.

This is a resting-state fMRI study comparing MDD patients and controls, not a task-based problem-solving or reasoning fMRI study. It examines long-term memory (Hurst exponent) of resting-state networks rather than task-evoked activations. Therefore it meets the resting-state exclusion criterion.

The study examines self- and other-reflection (metacognition/social cognition), not a problem-solving or reasoning task, so it fails inclusion criterion I2. Abstract does describe task-based fMRI and brain activation (I1, I4), but the task domain does not match the review objective. Healthy control sample is not clearly reported in abstract.
This study uses PET-FDG imaging in breast cancer patients (oncology patients), not task-based fMRI in healthy adults. It is a non-fMRI study and does not include healthy adult problem-solving tasks.

This is a review/methods paper on quantitative fMRI approaches in epilepsy rather than an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report task-related activations in a healthy adult sample.

This is a review article summarizing existing findings rather than a primary task-based fMRI experiment with human participants performing problem-solving tasks. It does not present new task-related fMRI activation results in healthy adults.

Abstract describes an fMRI study with stimulus-evoked BOLD responses (task-related) but does not indicate a problem-solving or reasoning task or clearly report participant sample details (healthy adults). Include for full-text review to confirm task type and sample.

Although this is a task-based fMRI study in healthy adults reporting activation, the task concerns social reputation/self-referential processing, not problem-solving or reasoning tasks required by the review objective.

This is a resting-state fMRI connectivity study of healthy participants; it does not involve a task-based problem-solving or reasoning task and reports resting-state connectivity rather than task-related activations. Excluded per E2 (Resting-state or connectivity-only studies).

This study reports task-based fMRI during a Go/No-Go response inhibition task in cognitively impaired multiple sclerosis patients; it does not include a healthy adult (18–65) sample and does not investigate problem-solving/reasoning tasks. Excluded for absence of healthy adult participants relevant to the review.

This is an animal (rat) PET and microdialysis pharmacology study of a serotonin transporter blocker, not a task-based fMRI study in humans performing problem-solving. It therefore lacks a healthy adult human sample and is not an fMRI task study.

Study recorded concurrent EEG-fMRI during resting wakefulness and analyzed intrinsic/ connectivity of alpha-band synchrony; no task-based problem-solving or reasoning paradigm was used, so it is a resting-state/connectivity study.

This article discusses PET and radiopharmaceuticals (FDG) and appears to be an overview; it is not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. Therefore it fails the non-fMRI criterion and appears to be a review.

This is a review of neuroimaging in ischemic stroke (penumbra) discussing PET/CT/perfusion MRI; it is not a task-based fMRI study, focuses on stroke/pathology rather than healthy adults, and reads as a review article. No task-related fMRI data in healthy adults are reported.

This fMRI study investigates auditory spatial perception/detection, not problem-solving or reasoning tasks. It does not meet the review’s core inclusion criterion I2 (problem-solving). Other inclusion criteria (task-based fMRI, healthy adult sample, and task-related activations) are present, but the task domain is outside the review scope.

Abstract reports task-based fMRI with task-related activation data (I1, I4). However, it studies instrumental learning/decision-making rather than an explicit problem-solving or reasoning task, and participant health/age is not specified. Insufficient detail to confirm I2 and I3, so include for full-text review.

This study uses resting-state fMRI and DTI to derive connectivity signatures and patient-control classification (MCI and schizophrenia). It is not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults and does not report task-related activations.

Abstract is a literature review summarizing EEG/fMRI findings on visual working memory and cortical maps, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It therefore meets the exclusion criterion for reviews/meta-analyses.
